equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: RitualNode no longer uses all default Ethereum builders directly; it installs RitualPayloadBuilderBuilder and RitualExecutorBuilder.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing RitualNode::components configuration that uses EthereumPayloadBuilder and EthereumExecutorBuilder directly with no Ritual wrapper builders.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/node/src/lib.rs uses EthereumPoolBuilder, EthereumNetworkBuilder, and EthereumConsensusBuilder, but payload is BasicPayloadServiceBuilder<RitualPayloadBuilderBuilder> and executor is RitualExecutorBuilder.
crates/node/src/executor.rs defines RitualExecutorBuilder with EthEvmConfig<RitualEvmFactory>, so the deleted-wrapper/all-Ethereum-builder invariant is no longer true.

rationale: The diff deletes the custom components module and changes the component builder type and construction calls from Ritual*Builder types to Ethereum*Builder types. The subject says all components were removed and eth is used for everything. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RitualNode::components and Node::ComponentsBuilder must use EthereumPoolBuilder, EthereumPayloadBuilder, EthereumNetworkBuilder, EthereumExecutorBuilder, and EthereumConsensusBuilder directly instead of Ritual wrapper builders.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate RitualNode::components in a compile-time or integration test and assert the configured component builder types are the Ethereum builders, with no dependency on the deleted Ritual component modules.
summary: RitualNode does not use all Ethereum builders directly in current nodebuilder code.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instantiate RitualNode::components in a compile-time assertion expecting EthereumPayloadBuilder/EthereumExecutorBuilder; current code should fail because it selects RitualPayloadBuilderBuilder/RitualExecutorBuilder.
title: RitualNode uses Ethereum builders
